name: SANKATTO: SHODAN DACHI (THREE CUTS: FIRST STANCE)

description.value:
The user drops in this stance and as part of the technique, draws and wields 3 of the weapon he chose when learning this stance -two in each hand, and one clenched between his teeth. The weapon must be a melee weapon that is the one below the user's size category or smaller, and could be feasibly held between the u sers' clenched teeth. Ultimately, the GM remains the final arbiter of what weapon can or cannot be used in this stance.

Entering this stance is a full-round action that may provoke an attack of opportunity. Should the user have the Quick Draw feat, the perform time is reduced to a move equivalent action, but still may provoke an attack of opportunity.

The user gains a single extra attack as part of this stance -using the weapon held in his mouth. This attack suffers a -4 penalty to hit due to the unorthodox manner, taken from either the users' highest attack bonus, or the relevant attack bonus if made as part of a full-attack action. The Two-Weapon fighting penalties apply, so a full attack action using 3 light weapons with a full-attack action of +20/+15/+10 would be +18/+18/+14/+13/+8 both the Two-Weapon Fighting and the -4 penalty stack.

Any attack made with the weapon held in the mouth suffers a -2 penalty to the damage roll. As always, Strength modifier applies, but can never benefit from being held in two hands. In addition, when rolling for opposed disarm checks, the user suffers an additional -2 penalty, on top of any other modifier to the roll.

This stance can be learned multiple times; each time it applies to different held weapon.

Requires: Two-Weapon Fighting (f), BaB +6

Material Focus: 3 held weapons of the same type.

Mastery: The first step of mastery reduces the penalty to-hit by -2, to a total of -2.

The second step of mastery reduces the penalty to damage by -1, to a total of weapon damage -1.

The third step of mastery reduces the penalty to-hit by a further -2, to a total of -0. This can only be learned at ECL 8.

The fourth step of mastery reduces the penalty to damage by a further -1, to a total of weapon damage.

The fifth step of mastery removes the penalty to disarm checks, and can only be learned at ECL 12. In addition, once per encounter, the user can spend an action point to enter this stance as a swift action.
